name: Flank Steak with Peppers Stir Fry
recipeIngredient: 1 Onion (medium sized), halved lengthwise, then cut into wedges
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the flank steak against the grain into 2-inch wide strips.
2. Cut each strip across the grain into 1/4-inch slices. For easier slicing, place the meat in the freezer for a short while before cutting.
3. In a medium bowl, combine the steak with 1 tablespoon soy sauce, 1 tablespoon vegetable oil, and 2 teaspoons cornstarch. Stir to coat the steak. Set aside.
4. Cut the bell pepper into thin slivers. Halve the onion lengthwise, then cut into 1/2-inch thick wedges.
5. Diagonally slice the celery into 1/2-inch slices. In a small bowl, stir together the beef broth, black pepper, and remaining 2 teaspoons cornstarch. Set aside.
6. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. When hot but not smoking, add the steak and marinade mixture. Stir-fry until the steak is browned and still slightly pink in the center, about 2 to 3 minutes.
7. Transfer the steak to a plate. Add the remaining tablespoon oil to the skillet. Add the pepper, onion, celery, and ginger. Stir-fry until onions begin to wilt, about 2 to 3 minutes.
8. Return the steak to the skillet. Stir in the broth mixture, bring to a boil, then cook, stirring constantly, until vegetables are crisp-tender and steak is cooked through, about 2 to 3 more minutes.
9. Serve hot with steamed rice.
Calories: 348
Protein: 23 gm
Fat: 25 gm
Carbo: 7 gm
Cholesterol: 59 gm
Sodium: 653 gm
